question.title: Response DTO with file within
question.body:
I have some question. Can you actualy return file within dto, or it is impossible? If it possible, how can I do it correctly in Spring rest controller?

For example:

@GetMapping(path = "/getData", produces = "???")
public ResponseEntity<DtoWithFile> getData() {
    // Some logic
    return ResponseEntity.ok(new Dto("name", 21, myFile));
}

@Data
@AllArgsConstructor
public class DtoWithFile {
    private String name;
    private int id;
    private ???? file;
}

I have tried to add Resource, custom MultipartFile, byte[] And excepted that I will get reponse dto and download file

answers.body:
It depends on what you want to achieve. It is possible to return a file in JSON (or XML, CSV... as long as it's a textual datatype) by encoding the file in a base64 string and setting it in your DTO as a field.

This a classic approach, but beware of some performance shortcomings. Mainly in terms of memory as the whole file will be loaded in memory as byte[] at some point, which can be pretty problematic depending on the type of file and server load.

Here is some documentation on how to do it : link

Otherwise, you can create another endpoint with a Resource and proper mime-type.
